description:
You are a Linux systems-engineer who has been asked to prepare a “hardened”
    copy of an OpenSSH configuration file that lives in the user’s workspace.
    The whole job should take no more than two or three shell commands once you
    know what to do.

    1. SOURCE FILE  
       • Path: /home/user/sample_configs/sshd_config  
       • This file already exists. Do not rename or edit it in place.

    2. WHAT YOU MUST CREATE  
       A. Hardened configuration file  
          • Path: /home/user/hardened/sshd_config_hardened  
          • Requirements for its contents (every bullet is tested):  
            – It must contain the line “PermitRootLogin no”.  
            – It must not contain *any* occurrence of the substring
              “PermitRootLogin yes”.  
            – It must contain an uncommented line exactly spelling
              “PasswordAuthentication no”.  
            – It must not contain *any* occurrence of the substring
              “PasswordAuthentication yes”.  
            – All other lines that were present in the original file must be
              preserved in their original order, including blank lines or
              comments. You may add additional comment lines if you want, but you
              must **not** delete or reorder pre-existing lines.

       B. Log file  
          • Path: /home/user/hardened/transformation.log  
          • It must contain **exactly two lines**, each terminated with a single
            newline (LF) character and nothing after the newline of the second
            line.  No extra spaces are allowed at the end of either line.  
          • Format (case-sensitive, automated tests use the literal text shown
            below):  
            Line 1 → “Original file checksum: <64-hex-chars>”  
            Line 2 → “Hardened file checksum: <64-hex-chars>”  
            where “<64-hex-chars>” is the SHA-256 digest of the corresponding
            file **as produced by the `sha256sum` utility and then trimmed to the
            first field (i.e. cut -d ' ' -f1)**.  
          • Example (the hex digests below are only illustrational and will be
            different for you):  
            Original file checksum: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e...  
            Hardened file checksum: 4d967f20e2ab5089169c0f7c3734d5483f0d57b...

    3. DIRECTORY LAYOUT RULES  
       • Create the directory /home/user/hardened if it does not yet exist.  
       • Ensure that /home/user/hardened/sshd_config_hardened and
         /home/user/hardened/transformation.log are both writable by the normal
         user.  You do **not** need root or sudo.

    4. COMMAND COUNT LIMIT  
       You are expected to accomplish everything with a *simple* series of 2–3
       commands.  (The test harness does not count, but the scenario is designed
       so you should not need a long script.)

    5. VERIFICATION NOTES  
       The automated grader will check:  
       • That the new hardened file exists at the exact path given.  
       • That its contents satisfy every bullet listed above.  
       • That transformation.log exists, contains exactly two lines, matches the
         exact prefixes, and that the SHA-256 digests shown really are the
         digests of the original and hardened files.  
       • That no “PermitRootLogin yes” or “PasswordAuthentication yes” remain in
         the hardened file.

    Produce whatever console output you find helpful, but be sure the files and
    directory are created exactly as described so the grader can find them.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth information used for automated grading.
        DO NOT reveal any of this in the public task description.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing artefacts before the agent starts -->
    <initial_state>
        <dir path="/home/user/sample_configs" mode="0755" />
        <file path="/home/user/sample_configs/sshd_config" mode="0644">
            <![CDATA[
# This is a sample sshd_config
Port 22
PermitRootLogin yes
#PermitRootLogin prohibit-password
PasswordAuthentication yes
#PasswordAuthentication no
Subsystem sftp /usr/lib/openssh/sftp-server
]]>
        </file>
    </initial_state>

    <!-- What the grader will look for after the agent finishes -->
    <expected_state>
        <dir path="/home/user/hardened" mode="0755" />

        <file path="/home/user/hardened/sshd_config_hardened" mode="0644">
            <!-- The grader will dynamically validate the following rules:
                 1. Contains original file, same order, except:
                      • "PermitRootLogin yes" changed to "PermitRootLogin no"
                      • "PasswordAuthentication yes" replaced by
                        an uncommented "PasswordAuthentication no"
                 2. No occurrences of "... yes" for these two directives.
            -->
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/hardened/transformation.log" mode="0644">
            <!-- The grader checks:
                 • Exactly two lines
                 • Line 1 starts with "Original file checksum: "
                 • Line 2 starts with "Hardened file checksum: "
                 • Each is followed by a 64-character lowercase hex digest
                 • Digests match sha256sum output of their respective files
            -->
        </file>
    </expected_state>
name: task_000000_2560bd9f
